statements.text: Static typing prevents fewer production bugs than its advocates claim.
Pattern matching improves readability less than it increases language complexity.
Backward compatibility is overrated when it blocks cleaner language design.
Null safety features often mask poor API design more than they prevent errors.
Generics add more accidental complexity than real expressive power.
A small standard library is better for ecosystems than an all-in-one one.
Most new syntax features optimize for conference talks, not maintainability.
The best language feature is the one that removes another feature.
